items.title: Mira Murati steps back into the spotlight, carefully
items.description: In the current environment, remaining heads down has diminishing returns; at some point, you have to make some noise just to remind the market you exist.

items.title: Ahead of its IPO, Anthropic’s Daniela Amodei shrugs off doubts about AI’s returns
items.description: Anthropic has been growing at a breakneck pace. The company announced that annualized revenue crossed $47 billion in May, up dramatically from roughly $9 billion at the end of 2025. That trajectory faces a real test, though.

items.title: Airbnb’s Brian Chesky plans to launch a new AI lab
items.description: The Airbnb CEO said last year it hasn't struck an LLM partnership because existing products weren't quite ready.

items.title: Defense tech, AI, and fundraising take center stage at StrictlyVC Los Angeles on June 18
items.description: On Thursday, June 18, at The Aerospace Corporation Campus, investors, founders, and tech leaders will gather for an evening of conversation exploring some of the most consequential shifts taking place across venture capital, defense technology, artificial intelligence, and advanced industry. Secure your spot today.

items.title: Meta steals a tactic from Tesla and builds data centers in tents
items.description: Meta may have found one way to slash its massive data center bill: tents.

items.title: Apple approves Poke as the first AI agent on its Messages for Business platform
items.description: Poke, the startup that lets people use AI agents through simple text messages, has become the first AI agent approved for Apple’s Messages for Business platform.

items.title: Meta rolls out a new AI creator assistant on Facebook
items.description: Creators often have to parse through charts and dashboards to understand their performance, but with the new AI assistant, they can get quick answers to questions like "When should I post?" and "What are people saying in my comments?"

items.title: What to expect from WWDC 2026: Siri’s highly anticipated revamp and Apple Intelligence updates
items.description: Apple's WWDC nears: Here's what you can look forward to.

items.title: Is Silicon Valley ready to put robots in people’s homes? Hello Robot is.
items.description: The California startup released the fourth-generation of its home assistance robot, Stretch.

items.title: Apple touts $1.4 trillion in App Store billings and sales, 90% without a commission
items.description: Apple's App Store generated $1.4 trillion in sales, up from $1.3 trillion last year, with $149 billion in sales for digital goods.

items.title: Lovable signs multiyear deal with Google Cloud to up usage 5x, source says
items.description: Lovable and Google signed an expanded multiyear deal that involves a 5x expansion of Lovable's footprint on Google Cloud, and expanded access to Anthropic Claude.

items.title: Alphabet’s record-breaking $85B raise for Google’s AI business is a helluva good signal
items.description: If Alphabet's record-breaking $85 billion stock sale signals investor appetite for AI-related offerings, we can see that investors are ready to chow.

items.title: Google’s Dreambeans, its weirdest-named AI tool to date, will turn your life into a cartoon
items.description: Dreambeans is a curated list of AI-illustrated "stories" culled from the personal data in your Google account.

items.title: Amazon will show AI product images when you search for some reason
items.description: Amazon will use visual search and AI to show AI-generated product images that match your search queries. The retailer says it will help guide users to products.

items.title: These two founders left Goldman and Meta to build voice AI for markets everyone else overlooked
items.description: The startup's own stack for Africa and Middle East is now handling more than 17,000 calls per day.

items.title: Publishers will be able to opt out of AI Search, thanks to new regulation
items.description: U.K. regulators are requiring Google offer a tool allowing website publishers to opt-out of generative AI search features. The option will be tested in the U.K. then rolled out globally.

items.title: Meta’s AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
items.description: WhatsApp will charge businesses for using its AI agent based on token usage.

items.title: Coralogix raises $200M on bet that someone needs to watch the AI agents
items.description: Coralogix is among a growing number of infrastructure firms betting that as AI systems move into production, demand will rise for tools that can monitor their behavior, troubleshoot failures, and provide the operational data needed to keep them running reliably.

items.title: Cyera eyes $12B valuation at 80x ARR multiple despite operating losses
items.description: The cybersecurity company is nearing a $300 million round led by Evolution Equity Partners.

items.title: Uber caps employee AI spending after blowing through budget in 4 months
items.description: Uber's cutback has occurred after the company had reportedly encouraged staff to use AI as much as possible.

items.title: The AI IPO Race Heats Up, DOGE Whistleblower Sues Elon Musk, and Instagram Gets Hacked
items.description: On Uncanny Valley, we dive into the IPO bonanza that the top AI companies are embarking on to the point where some real estate listings are looking for not just regular old cash, but Anthropic stock.

items.title: Jeff Bezos Is Funding a Wild Hunt for the Brain’s ‘Core Algorithm’
items.description: With $500 million in funding and a reported $2.5 billion valuation, Flourish wants to reinvent AI by putting real neurons under the microscope.

items.title: Alpha School’s Ritzy New York City Campus Costs $65,000 a Year—but Isn’t Actually a School
items.description: A homeschooling center in Manhattan is part of the company’s nationwide expansion. Internal documents reveal its strategy: “Opening date > safety.”

items.title: Quantum Computing Is Having Its Public Market Moment
items.description: Quantinuum, a quantum computing startup, is losing millions. Investors want in anyway.

items.title: OpenAI and Anthropic Sign Letter to Prevent AI-Developed Biological Weapons
items.description: Leading AI labs, executives, and scientists are sending a letter to lawmakers urging them to improve tracking of synthetic DNA sequences that could be used for bioweapons.

items.title: xAI Asks Court to Strip Alleged Grok Deepfake Nudes Victims of Anonymity
items.description: Four people suing Elon Musk's AI firm under pseudonyms due to the risks of being identified may face a difficult choice: Reveal your real names, or drop the lawsuit.

items.title: The Humanoid Robot of the Future Is a 6-Foot-Tall Beefcake With a Chinese Body and an American Brain
items.description: Spencer Huang, Nvidia’s robotics lead, tells WIRED that the new bot combines the best of both worlds.

items.title: Meta Business Agent drives AI-powered conversational commerce
items.description:
Meta has launched Business Agent to automate conversational commerce workflows directly inside its messaging applications. The software allows global retail brands to execute transactions and field support tickets without human intervention. Deploying this architecture places agentic AI directly at the core of social commerce. Meta integrated these workflows natively into Instagram, Messenger, and soon WhatsApp. […]
